mild: Most folk think it's nothing. I'm not so sure.
Everyone's got a theory. None of them agree.
People around here pretend everything's fine. That's how they cope.
The children have started making up songs about it. That's how you know it's sunk in.
The tavern's never been busier. People don't want to be alone with their thoughts.
We had a meeting about it. Lasted three hours. Decided absolutely nothing.
Funny thing is, the ones who panic the loudest are usually the last to actually do anything.
Word travels fast in a small place. Faster than the truth, usually.
We've survived worse. At least that's what we keep telling each other.
Some people care a lot. Others just shrug and carry on.
It's the sort of thing everyone has an opinion on but nobody does anything about.
The older folk remember when it was different. The younger ones don't see what the fuss is about.
Ask five people and you'll get six answers. That's this settlement for you.
intense: The elders won't talk about it, which worries me more than the thing itself.
Half the town wants to investigate. The other half wants to pack up and leave.
The farmers are spooked, and when the farmers are spooked, you pay attention.
Nobody wants to be the first to say it out loud, so we all just don't.
There's been talk of sending someone to look into it. Nobody's volunteered.
The merchants stopped coming through last week. They know something we don't.
Old Maren says she's seen this before. She also says she hoped she never would again.
Some folk have started sleeping in shifts. I thought they were being dramatic until last night.
A few families left quietly in the night. Can't say I blame them.
The guard captain says it's under control. The guard captain is a terrible liar.
There's a difference between being calm and being in denial. This town chose denial.
